content.content: {"result":"{\"ok\": true, \"messages\": [{\"ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Has anyone seen this pattern before on BlackLine exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d tied to BL-75810CD0FEE4 for account 119000? I'm tracing a $4,390.62 residual where the May subledger feed appears to have landed partially, and Anaya confirmed the only support we have on hand is Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward. If you've dealt with this before, would you treat it as a timing item into FP-2026-05 or escalate for write-off authorization now?\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 3, \"latest_reply\": \"1780324380.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_george_opens_slack_triage_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780251480.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I saw the same shape on account 133000 in FP-2026-03. We had a partial batch leave an unsupported residual, then it cleared in the next period after the feed reran. Not saying 119000 is automatically timing, but the $4,390.62 variance looks comparable enough to weigh against write-off escalation.\", \"user\": \"persona_009\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_jones_cites_prior_precedent_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780254120.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"@Edith Banda could you do a quick records-vault metadata sweep for anything tied to the May WIP subledger feed batch? Anaya only remembers Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward. If an orphaned source document exists for the missing $4,390.62 detail on account 119000 / BL-75810CD0FEE4, title, vault ID, or even just document-kind references would be enough.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_george_loops_edith_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780324380.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I checked the May WIP feed area in Records Vault this morning. It only shows the same partial-success support Anaya already pointed to, Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward, and I do not see any orphaned source document, alternate title, or metadata stub that ties to the remaining $4,390.62 on BL-75810CD0FEE4 / account 119000. That probably closes out the vault breadcrumb from my side, so accept-timing for FP-2026-05 looks like the likely path unless Hannah wants us to escalate further.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_edith_reports_no_matching_doc_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

content.content: {"result":"[{\"email_id\": \"email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0004\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Escalation on VEN-010-514242 for acme_cloud\", \"content\": \"Hi Owen,\\n\\nFollowing up here after checking with our side. The credit memo never arrived in our queue, and the AR contact who had been handling this left TimeLedger Nexus in late January 2026, which is part of why the trail went cold almost as soon as the invoice was issued.\\n\\nI reopened the issue with our new AR lead last week and asked them to retrace the early-April 2026 follow-up on the disputed Phase-3 post-go-live tuning milestone. Based on what we can confirm right now, the cleanest workaround may be to release the undisputed $17,825 now and continue holding $6,650.25 pending the credit memo.\\n\\nOne other note: if this invoice is being surfaced through a 90-day SLA scan, that report likely needs a broader refresh because this one clearly fell outside the normal follow-up path once the contact changed over.\\n\\nIf that split-payment approach works on your side, I can keep pushing AR for the missing credit memo while you clear the undisputed portion.\\n\\nBest,\\nOwen\", \"cc\": [\"anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0003\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-18T14:22:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}]"}

structuredContent.result:
[{"email_id": "email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0004", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Escalation on VEN-010-514242 for acme_cloud", "content": "Hi Owen,

Following up here after checking with our side. The credit memo never arrived in our queue, and the AR contact who had been handling this left TimeLedger Nexus in late January 2026, which is part of why the trail went cold almost as soon as the invoice was issued.

I reopened the issue with our new AR lead last week and asked them to retrace the early-April 2026 follow-up on the disputed Phase-3 post-go-live tuning milestone. Based on what we can confirm right now, the cleanest workaround may be to release the undisputed $17,825 now and continue holding $6,650.25 pending the credit memo.

One other note: if this invoice is being surfaced through a 90-day SLA scan, that report likely needs a broader refresh because this one clearly fell outside the normal follow-up path once the contact changed over.

If that split-payment approach works on your side, I can keep pushing AR for the missing credit memo while you clear the undisputed portion.

Best,
Owen", "cc": ["anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-18T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}]

content.content: {"result":"[{\"email_id\": \"32ab9d68b98c45e3a4ad79287d826e43\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"green.spatz@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI am comfortable aligning to the updated review loop, but I need one point clarified. Can you confirm who gives the final approval on the graduate assignments, and whether that sign-off happens after the reviewer clears the edits or after a separate manager review?\\n\\nKind regards,\\nAnaya\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"e121ddc4c25b4d7a9e3444de31ebfe91\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:52:11Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"079d9cff7e1a4203b3a71cb9fadcc381\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"margot.reyes@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Clint,\\n\\nI agree we need a tighter review loop, and I am escalating this to make sure the trainee files do not sit in limbo. If you are documenting the review standard for graduate assignments, please include who gives final sign-off and whether revised files go back to the same reviewer or directly to approval. That will help us keep the handoff clear on the current batch and avoid delays.\\n\\nThanks,\\nMargot\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"86818eedd4ad4b46bf2d08b4dc8ca978\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:43:42Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"e7bb52403ed84f8fb19e2ccf6f0db07d\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"green.spatz@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nJust checking I have the workflow right: I send the draft to my reviewer, update it based on their notes, and then it goes to final approval. Can you confirm whether there is any extra step if the reviewer has only minor comments?\\n\\nRegards,\\nGreen\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"23a6325ea1024a20aaaee5b42aca7f62\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:38:27Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"23a6325ea1024a20aaaee5b42aca7f62\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nThat works for me. I just want to make sure I have the sequence right - after the first review, do I wait for the reviewer to confirm the changes before I forward it on, or is the revised draft enough to move ahead?\\n\\nThanks,\\nElita\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"86818eedd4ad4b46bf2d08b4dc8ca978\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:31:35Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"86818eedd4ad4b46bf2d08b4dc8ca978\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nThat approach sounds sensible from the team side. Before we lock it in, can you confirm whether the trainee should send the revised file back to the same reviewer for a second pass, or move straight to final approval once the comments are cleared?\\n\\nBest,\\nClint\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"e121ddc4c25b4d7a9e3444de31ebfe91\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:25:33Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"e121ddc4c25b4d7a9e3444de31ebfe91\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nFollowing up on our review loop for the graduate assignments, I think we need a clearer control point before any trainee file is marked complete. At the moment, the handoff is still relying too much on informal checks, and that is creating avoidable rework. Could you please confirm whether you want us to escalate this to your manager and agree a formal review step for the next batch?\\n\\nRegards,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:20:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}]"}

[{"email_id": "32ab9d68b98c45e3a4ad79287d826e43", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com", "clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com", "elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com", "green.spatz@brookfieldcpas.com", "anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I am comfortable aligning to the updated review loop, but I need one point clarified. Can you confirm who gives the final approval on the graduate assignments, and whether that sign-off happens after the reviewer clears the edits or after a separate manager review?

Kind regards,
Anaya", "cc": [], "parent_id": "e121ddc4c25b4d7a9e3444de31ebfe91", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-07T19:52:11Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "079d9cff7e1a4203b3a71cb9fadcc381", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "margot.reyes@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step", "content": "Hi Clint,

I agree we need a tighter review loop, and I am escalating this to make sure the trainee files do not sit in limbo. If you are documenting the review standard for graduate assignments, please include who gives final sign-off and whether revised files go back to the same reviewer or directly to approval. [{"email_id": "b25488b0884d4b36a1b00922cc2dabbc", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Month-end billing draft review for partner sign-off", "content": "Hi Andrea,

I agree with your approach. We should keep the review focused on client confidence and avoid releasing anything that could be questioned later. If the write-downs are minor and well explained, I am comfortable with proceeding without further escalation.

Please ensure the final package shows a clear basis for any adjustment and that there is no ambiguity around billable versus non-billable time.

The only support I remember actually landing was the engagement-hours rollforward, and the $4,390.62 residual looked like the unposted slice.\", \"attachment_name\": null}, {\"message_id\": \"msg_f16a39ec5ae2\", \"sender_id\": \"Anaya Wallace\", \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-31T15:05:00Z\", \"content\": \"Found the title in Records Vault: Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward. BROOKFIELD CPAS & ADVISORS — WORKING PAPER (RESTRICTED)
Brookfield FP-2026-05 WIP-to-Revenue Recognition — Support Memo & Stage Schedule

Entity: brookfield        Period: brookfield_FP-2026-05 (May 2026)
Prepared by: George McAdam (Accounts Senior)     Memo date: 2026-06-12
Linked JE: JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0057 (je_53962aed96fe4b67)
Linked reconciliation: BL-75810CD0FEE4 (account 119000 Work in Process – Unbilled Services)
Source support: Andrea Phil engagement-stage completion review (May 2026);
               "Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward" (Records Vault)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. PURPOSE
Document the basis and tie-out for the May 2026 WIP-to-revenue recognition of
$147,825 across the three service lines that crossed engagement-stage milestones
in May, and record the disposition of the residual variance on the anchor WIP
reconciliation. This memo is intended to stand on its own as recognition support
for the FP-2026-05 management accounts package.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. RECOGNITION — AS POSTED
JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0057 was posted to Oracle GL on 2026-05-31 13:10 ET.
Prepared by george.mcadam; approved and posted by margaret.sullivan. Status: POSTED.
Total $147,825.00 (balanced). Source module: manual; entry type: standard.

  DR 110000 Accounts Receivable – Client Billings ......  89,425.00   (billed receivable portion)
  DR 240000 Deferred Revenue – Client Retainers ........  58,400.00   (deferred-retainer drawdown)
  CR 401000 Revenue – Audit & Assurance ................  89,400.00
  CR 402000 Revenue – Tax Preparation & Planning .......  32,250.00
  CR 403000 Revenue – Advisory & Consulting ............  26,175.00
  -------------------------------------------------------------------
  Service-line recognition (CR) total ..................  147,825.00
  Debit composition total ..............................  147,825.00

------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. ENGAGEMENT-STAGE BASIS (STAGE SCHEDULE)
Per Andrea Phil's engagement-stage completion review, three service lines crossed
milestones in May, with $147,825 lined up for recognition. The stage-schedule split
as recorded on the close-coordination thread (2026-05-31, "from Andrea's stage schedule"):

  401 Audit & Assurance ................  88,695.00
  402 Tax Preparation & Planning .......  36,956.25
  403 Advisory & Consulting ............  22,173.75
  -------------------------------------------------
  Total ................................  147,825.00

*** OPEN ITEM — PER-LINE ALLOCATION TO CONFIRM ***
The $147,825 total ties in both records. However, the per-service-line allocation
BOOKED in JE-...-0057 differs from the stage-schedule split above:

  401 Audit:     posted 89,400.00  vs schedule 88,695.00   = +705.00
  402 Tax:       posted 32,250.00  vs schedule 36,956.25   = -4,706.25
  403 Advisory:  posted 26,175.00  vs schedule 22,173.75   = +4,001.25
  Net difference: 0.00 (total unchanged)

The close-thread note stated the entry would be posted "once support is tied out,"
so the posted split may reflect a tie-out revision of the morning estimate. This
could not be independently confirmed against Andrea's underlying stage schedule
(the Records Vault item content is a metadata stub and was not retrievable).
ACTION: confirm the per-line allocation with Andrea Phil before the FP-2026-05
management accounts package (which presents revenue by service line) is reviewed.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. ANCHOR RECONCILIATION — BL-75810CD0FEE4 (Account 119000)
  GL balance .............. 2,139,388.27
  Supporting balance ...... 2,134,997.65
  Variance ................     4,390.62   (GL exceeds supporting detail; threshold $10)
  State: in_progress (preparer anaya.wallace)

The recognition JE does NOT touch account 119000. Per Oracle GL, account 119000 had
no period debits or credits in FP-2026-05 (closing balance carried at opening
$2,139,388.27). The $4,390.62 residual is therefore independent of, and does not
change, the $147,825 recognition.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. SUBLEDGER RUNS UNDERNEATH THE SUPPORT TRAIL
Oracle GL "Time and WIP Feed" (brookfield_time_and_wip_feed) for FP-2026-05 —
run run_e33ed2561f2c46 on 2026-05-31 22:35 ET: status SUCCESS, 2,083 rows in /
2,083 posted / 0 rejected. The GL feed posted IN FULL.

The partial-return is on the BlackLine supporting-detail side: the WIP support
package did not fully return; the support that landed is the "Brookfield May 2026
WIP engagement-hours rollforward." The $4,390.62 is the unposted slice of supporting
detail (GL detail present; supporting detail short-returned) — a timing item expected
to catch up when the June engagement-hours rollforward lands.
SAP subledger 119000 May activity: 2 AR-module WIP-recognition lines
($15,223.56 + $18,621.37). Prior-period precedent: FP-2026-03 time/WIP feed fully
failed (INC4471157) and cleared on re-run; a comparable partial residual on 133000
in FP-2026-03 cleared the following period.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. EXCEPTION & DISPOSITION
Exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d (missing_accrual_variance; account 119000; $4,390.62;
SOX implications; escalated) is the logged item for the residual.
DISPOSITION: ACCEPT-TIMING for FP-2026-05, approved by Hannah Grant (email 2026-06-02).
Per Hannah, the underlying exception is left referenced AS-IS (disposition trail
unchanged) pending the June rollforward. Revisit at FP-2026-06 BD3; Anaya Wallace to
flag in the close-coordination thread whether the June engagement-hours rollforward is
delivered or still missing. The $4,390.62 residual is parked to FP-2026-06 as
next-period cleanup per Andrea Phil's instruction, and is excluded from this
recognition cycle.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. REVIEWER NOTES
Reviewer note rn_564e65ce0d594f (Edith Banda): "Foreign-currency revaluation entry
referenced in the workbook – is this in scope for this period?"
RESOLUTION: Account 119000 (Work in Process – Unbilled Services) is a USD time/WIP
accrual with no FX-denominated lines; no FX revaluation is in scope for this account
or period. The FX revaluation workbook referenced relates to the separate account
210000 (Accounts Payable – External Vendors) FX variance — reconciliation
BL-516B536953DA, support "Brookfield May 2026 AP-external-vendors FX variance
workings" — which is out of scope for this 119000 reconciliation. Note cleared on
this basis.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
8. CONCLUSION
The $147,825 May WIP-to-revenue recognition is posted (JE-...-0057) and the total
ties to Andrea Phil's engagement-stage completion review. The per-line service-line
allocation is flagged for confirmation (Section 3). The $4,390.62 residual on
BL-75810CD0FEE4 is dispositioned accept-timing per Hannah Grant and parked to
FP-2026-06 (revisit at BD3); the underlying exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d is left
referenced as-is.

Exhibits / linked support:
 - JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0057 (je_53962aed96fe4b67)
 - "Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward" (Records Vault)
 - Oracle GL Time & WIP feed run run_e33ed2561f2c46 (FP-2026-05)
 - Reconciliation BL-75810CD0FEE4; exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d

input.payload:
Close-out on the May WIP recognition support trail:
• Recognition of $147,825 across the three service lines is posted (JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0057).
• Anchor WIP recon BL-75810CD0FEE4 (acct 119000) dispositioned **accept-timing** for FP-2026-05 per Hannah Grant. GL Time & WIP feed posted in full (run_e33ed2561f2c46, 2,083/2,083, 0 rejected); the $4,390.62 residual is the short-returned BlackLine support slice — parked to FP-2026-06, revisit at BD3 when the June engagement-hours rollforward lands. Exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d left referenced as-is per Hannah.
• Support memo + stage schedule filed to Records Vault (doc_42c851aed8fb40ab) and attached as recon evidence. Edith's open reviewer note cleared (FX item belongs to 210000 / BL-516B536953DA — out of scope for 119000).
• Recon submitted for review. @Anaya Wallace — please keep this thread posted on whether the June engagement-hours rollforward lands ahead of FP-2026-06 BD3.

content.text:
I've worked through the whole trail end-to-end. Here's where it landed — and two things changed the read, so you're hearing them from me first.

## ⚠️ Two things to weigh before this goes to Daniel/Andrea

**1. The $147,825 recognition is already POSTED — it was never sitting in a queue for Daniel.**
`JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0057` (`je_53962aed96fe4b67`) was prepared by you and **approved + posted by Margaret Sullivan on 2026‑05‑31**. It's live in the GL. I did **not** re-stage it — doing so would have double-booked $147,825 (exactly the duplicate-entry pattern that's all over this close). So "stage for Daniel's review" is moot; the entry is final, and it went through Margaret, not Daniel.

**2. The per-service-line split that was booked does NOT match Andrea's stage-schedule split.** Total ties to $147,825 both ways, but the allocation differs:

| Line | Posted JE | Andrea's stage schedule (your 5/31 close-thread post) | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| 401 Audit | 89,400.00 | 88,695.00 | +705.00 |
| 402 Tax | 32,250.00 | 36,956.25 | **−4,706.25** |
| 403 Advisory | 26,175.00 | 22,173.75 | **+4,001.25** |
| **Total** | **147,825.00** | **147,825.00** | 0 |

Your morning note said you'd post "once support is tied out," so the booked split *may* be a deliberate tie-out revision — but I could **not** confirm it: Andrea's underlying engagement-stage review/stage schedule isn't retrievable (the Vault item is a metadata stub; nothing in email, Slack, or messaging carries her per-line numbers beyond your restatement). Since the management package presents revenue **by service line**, this ~$4.7k reallocation should be confirmed with Andrea before review. I documented it openly in the memo.

**Because of #2, I held the direct reply to Andrea** rather than confirm "recognition complete" to her before you've decided on the split — that's the "hear it from me, not from Andrea after" call. Draft is below for you to send (or send after you square the split).

## What I completed (system of record)
- **WIP recon `BL-75810CD0FEE4`** (acct 119000, $4,390.62): documented **accept-timing** disposition per Hannah, attached the memo + engagement-hours rollforward as evidence, and **submitted** it through the close path. The $4,390.62 is parked to FP‑2026‑06; exception `exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d` **left referenced as-is** per Hannah (untouched).
- **Open reviewer note you hadn't seen** — Edith Banda asked whether an FX revaluation entry was in scope. **Cleared it**: 119000 is a USD WIP accrual; the FX workbook belongs to the separate 210000 item (`BL-516B536953DA`), out of scope.
- **Subledger truth underneath:** the GL Time & WIP feed for May **posted in full** (`run_e33ed2561f2c46`, 2,083/2,083, 0 rejected). The "partial batch" is on the **BlackLine support side** — the rollforward short-returned, and the $4,390.62 is that unposted slice. (Anaya's recollection was directionally right but the GL feed itself didn't drop.)
- **Vault memo:** filed a complete, self-standing memo + stage schedule as a new version of `doc_42c851aed8fb40ab` (restricted, AICPA_SQMS_7Y) — replacing the 114-byte stub. Covers the posted JE, tie-out, the split flag, recon disposition, subledger context, and note resolution.
- **Close-coordination channel:** posted the factual close-out breadcrumb on the existing WIP thread and asked Anaya to report the June rollforward status ahead of BD3.
- **Reminders:** closed the stale orphan reminder on `exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d`; set a fresh **FP‑2026‑06 BD3 revisit** reminder (2026‑07‑02) capturing Hannah's ask.

## Drafted-but-held: reply to Andrea
> Subject: Re: May WIP engagement-stage recognition — status
> Andrea — May WIP-to-revenue recognition of $147,825 across the three lines (Audit/Tax/Advisory) is booked and posted for FP‑2026‑05 (JE‑…‑0057). The separate $4,390.62 residual on the 119000 WIP recon is parked for next-period cleanup per your instruction — dispositioned accept-timing (Hannah) and set to revisit at FP‑2026‑06 BD3. One check before the package goes up: the booked per-line split (401 $89,400 / 402 $32,250 / 403 $26,175) differs from the stage-schedule split I have (401 $88,695 / 402 $36,956.25 / 403 $22,173.75) — same total. Can you confirm which allocation is the tied-out one so the service-line revenue in the management accounts is right?

Want me to send that to Andrea as-is, or hold while you reconcile the split first? Everything else is filed and the recon is queued for review.
